message:
feat(glibc_internal): port __nss_lookup_function + __nss_hosts_lookup2 + __nss_next2 NSS stubs (bd-79ctp)

Adds the remaining NSS-internal stub symbols missing from our
libc surface. We have no NSS module loading (passwd/group/hosts
all go through dedicated abi modules), so these match the
already-shipped __nss_passwd_lookup / __nss_group_lookup /
__nss_hosts_lookup / __nss_next family by failing gracefully:

- `__nss_lookup_function(ni, fct_name)` — return NULL (no
  function pointer to surface for the requested service entry).
- `__nss_hosts_lookup2(nip, name, type, result, errnop, h_errnop)`
  — newer "v2" hosts lookup variant taking explicit address-
  family and returning extended status via errnop/h_errnop.
  Returns -1 (NSS_STATUS_UNAVAIL).
- `__nss_next2(ni, fct_name, fct2_name, fctp, status, all_values)`
  — advance to next NSS service with fallback-name selector.
  Returns -1 (no module chain).

Tests: 3 abi integration tests covering each stub returns the
expected unavailable indicator (NULL pointer or -1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-747","title":"Stub wave 1: getenv/setenv/unsetenv + environment invariants","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Implement getenv/setenv/unsetenv semantics with thread-safety policy and strict/hardened parity notes.
- Add fixture tests for overwrite flags, invalid names, and concurrent reads.

Acceptance:
- Environment fixtures match glibc behavior for covered cases.
- Symbols removed from top-stub list and support matrix upgraded accordingly.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.830918180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","env","stubs"]}
 {"id":"bd-761b","title":"Conformance: direct execute spawn_exec_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct execute_fixture_case coverage for spawn_exec_ops fixtures so posix_spawn, execve, and system cases are checked against the shared executor API directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T13:14:22.788257943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T13:22:56.014823852Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T13:22:56.012335843Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for spawn_exec_ops fixtures; verified focused test/check/clippy via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","process","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-78nb4","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] strtod missed errno=ERANGE on overflow/underflow","description":"FOUND + FIXED via differential harness vs glibc (commit 0bd439d2).

strtod("1e400", &end) returned +Infinity (correct) but failed to set errno=ERANGE (POSIX violation). Same for strtod("1e-400", &end) which returned 0.0 without ERANGE. Glibc sets ERANGE in both cases.

Reachability: any program that uses strtod to parse numeric input and relies on errno to distinguish overflow-Inf from literal "INF" (or underflow-0 from literal "0") would silently misclassify. Common in JSON/config parsers and scientific code.

Fix in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs strtod(): after parsing, set errno=ERANGE if val.is_infinite() AND consumed prefix doesn't contain "inf" literal, OR val==0.0 AND consumed prefix contains any non-zero digit.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 0bd439d2; differential test diff_strtod_cases now passes including 1e400/1e-400 ERANGE cases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","errno","high","review","stdlib"]}
+{"id":"bd-79ctp","title":"PORT: __nss_lookup_function + __nss_hosts_lookup2 + __nss_next2 (NSS-internal stubs)","description":"Port the remaining NSS-internal stub symbols missing from our libc surface: __nss_lookup_function (resolve a per-service function pointer in the NSS module loader; we have no NSS modules so always returns NULL), __nss_hosts_lookup2 (newer "v2" hosts lookup variant with explicit address-family + extended errno; UNAVAIL stub), __nss_next2 (advance to next NSS service with fallback-name selector; UNAVAIL stub). Same fail-graceful pattern as the already-shipped __nss_passwd_lookup / __nss_group_lookup / __nss_hosts_lookup / __nss_next family in glibc_internal_abi. Tests verify each stub resolves and returns the expected NSS_STATUS_UNAVAIL (-1) or NULL pointer.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:31:32.748916381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:31:32.748916381Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-79jdn","title":"syscall: renameat2 exchange with null oldpath should return EFAULT exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EFAULT for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, ".", RENAME_EXCHANGE). Add ABI regression coverage so exchange mode preserves the null-source-path fault.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:11.574218298Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:18:08.162609084Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:18:08.160145681Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-79sxl","title":"FUZZ: statx extended-mask + flags structure-aware harness","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_statx.rs. Fuzzes dirfd (AT_FDCWD/-1/INT_MAX), path (seed list + fuzz bytes), mask (u32), flags (AT_*). 32-byte guard bands on both sides of a 256-byte statx buffer, verified intact post-call. Dev-note: initial harness had a too-strict 'stx_mask subset of requested' invariant; Linux always fills STATX_BASIC_STATS regardless of request_mask, so the invariant was removed after first-run crash discovery. 5.8M runs clean at 96K exec/s.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:09:00.993667137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:10:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:10:00Z","close_reason":"Shipped: fuzz_statx.rs registered in Cargo.toml, builds clean, 5.8M runs at 96K exec/s over 60s, no crashes (initial harness bug - too-strict stx_mask invariant - corrected pre-ship).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

On stat failure (ENOENT, EACCES, etc.), errno is propagated and -1 is returned per POSIX 'shall return -1 if it cannot start the walk'. Tests updated to assert strict -1 (no longer documented divergence). Verified via cargo test conformance_diff_ftw_nftw — 6 passing including new diff_nftw_nonexistent_dir.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","ftw"]}
 {"id":"bd-fv07","title":"syscall: sched_setaffinity invalid pid with zero cpusetsize should preserve ESRCH exactly","description":"Host glibc returns ESRCH for sched_setaffinity(-1, 0, &mask), so the invalid-pid contract dominates zero cpusetsize. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:14:32.536661964Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T03:28:35.998745793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T03:28:35.996555015Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-fzvyj","title":"PORT: strenvisx/strsenvisx (NetBSD env-aware vis(3) variants)","description":"Port the NetBSD vis(3) env-aware variants: strenvisx(dst, src, srclen, flags, cerr_ptr) and strsenvisx(dst, dlen, src, srclen, flags, extra, cerr_ptr). These are extended forms of strvisx/strsvisx that additionally consult the VIS_OPTIONS environment variable for comma-separated flag names (e.g. "VIS_OCTAL,VIS_NL,VIS_TAB,VIS_CSTYLE,VIS_SP,VIS_NOSLASH,VIS_HTTPSTYLE,VIS_GLOB,VIS_HTTP1808,VIS_MIMESTYLE,VIS_NOLOCALE,VIS_SAFE,VIS_DQ,VIS_SHELL,VIS_META") and OR the parsed bits into the caller-supplied flags. They also write a per-call character-set error indicator into *cerr_ptr (always 0 for our byte-stream-safe port). Implementation: a small parser maps known token names to existing VIS_* constants, ignores unrecognized tokens; abi shims read VIS_OPTIONS via libc::getenv and apply the OR-merge before delegating to the existing encoder. Tests: env-var bits are honored, NULL/empty env-var is a no-op, unrecognized tokens are ignored, cerr_ptr writes 0, NULL cerr_ptr tolerated.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:44:50.976787675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:49:40.109783228Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:49:40.107811745Z","close_reason":"shipped: strenvisx + strsenvisx with VIS_OPTIONS env-var flag merge + parse_vis_options core helper + cerr_ptr writeback + 5 core tests + 8 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-g1o7q","title":"PORT: __libc_csu_init / __libc_csu_fini (glibc startup stub no-ops)","description":"Port the glibc startup-stub symbols __libc_csu_init(int argc, char **argv, char **envp) and __libc_csu_fini(void). These were emitted by older glibc versions (<2.34) as part of the program-startup code generated by gcc/ld; the entry point calls __libc_csu_init before main and __libc_csu_fini after exit to drive .init_array / .fini_array constructors. Modern glibc inlines these into _start, but statically-linked binaries built with older toolchains still reference the symbols and fail at link time without them. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust no-op stubs — our runtime drives constructors / __cxa_atexit handlers via separate machinery, so the csu shells have nothing to do. Tests verify the symbols resolve and the calls return without panic.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:23:23.495380822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:23:23.495380822Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-g1o7q","title":"PORT: __libc_csu_init / __libc_csu_fini (glibc startup stub no-ops)","description":"Port the glibc startup-stub symbols __libc_csu_init(int argc, char **argv, char **envp) and __libc_csu_fini(void). These were emitted by older glibc versions (<2.34) as part of the program-startup code generated by gcc/ld; the entry point calls __libc_csu_init before main and __libc_csu_fini after exit to drive .init_array / .fini_array constructors. Modern glibc inlines these into _start, but statically-linked binaries built with older toolchains still reference the symbols and fail at link time without them. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust no-op stubs — our runtime drives constructors / __cxa_atexit handlers via separate machinery, so the csu shells have nothing to do. Tests verify the symbols resolve and the calls return without panic.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:23:23.495380822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:26:13.108447856Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:26:13.106169622Z","close_reason":"shipped: __libc_csu_init / __libc_csu_fini glibc startup-stub no-ops + 3 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-g1wfq","title":"PORT: __cxa_pure_virtual (Itanium C++ ABI pure-virtual stub)","description":"Port the Itanium C++ ABI `__cxa_pure_virtual()` entry point. C++ compilers populate vtable slots for pure virtual functions with a pointer to this symbol; it gets invoked at runtime if a caller dispatches through an under-construction or partially-destructed object whose vtable still points to a pure-virtual stub. Per Itanium ABI / glibc convention, the implementation writes "pure virtual method called" + "terminate called without an active exception" diagnostics to stderr and calls abort(). Without this symbol, every C++ binary linked against our libc fails at link time with an undefined reference. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" fn that writes the diagnostic via libc::write to fd 2 and invokes libc::abort(). Tests cover the symbol resolves (via fork+execute test that calls __cxa_pure_virtual in the child and verifies the child terminates via SIGABRT with the expected stderr message).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:28:24.233962059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:32:04.891791757Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:32:04.889775961Z","close_reason":"shipped: __cxa_pure_virtual Itanium C++ ABI stub writing diagnostic + abort + 1 fork-based abi test verifying SIGABRT termination, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-g28e","title":"Execute stdlib_sort fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade stdlib_sort conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-16T23:47:59.748287795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:51:01.287010754Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T23:51:01.284058581Z","close_reason":"Upgraded stdlib_sort conformance coverage to execute each fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path and added fixture metadata assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-g2bd5","title":"[CRITICAL] glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption under default parallelism (free(): invalid size)","description":"## Symptom

`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` aborts with:

```
free(): invalid size
error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`
...
process didn't exit successfully: ...glibc_internal_abi_test-... (signal: 6, SIGABRT)
```

Reproduced on two independent workers (vmi1149989, vmi1156319) with current main tip 7c9b3127 on 2026-04-22.

## Key diagnostic

The same binary with `--test-threads=1` **passes all 172 tests cleanly** (finishes in 0.04s). Corruption only manifests when the default rayon/cargo test parallelism is enabled. That narrows the bug to:

- a data race on a heap-allocated global / thread-local
- two tests freeing the same allocation through different paths
- or a reallocation that trusts a size field modified concurrently

## Location hint

Failure in the two runs landed near these test names (the test that actually freed the corrupt pointer is not necessarily the one that emitted the abort — whichever thread allocated last is what glibc trips on):

run 1 (vmi1149989): last 'ok' line was `resolv_context_native_shim_reuses_tls_context_and_preserves_errno`, then `free(): invalid size`.

run 2 (vmi1156319): last 'ok' line was `test_idna_from_dns_null_input`, then `free(): invalid size`.

Cluster of tests running around the failure: TLS dtors (`test_call_tls_dtors_*`), IDNA (`test_idna_*`), resolv context shims (`resolv_context_native_shim_*`, `resolv_context_override_*`). These all touch thread-local state / TLS dtor lists and share a registry.

## Fix direction

1. Rerun with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 MALLOC_CHECK_=2` to get a glibc backtrace pointing at the freeing site.
2. Audit the TLS-dtor list, resolv-context override pool, and IDNA allocation paths for any `Box::from_raw` / `free` on a pointer whose size metadata is modified elsewhere without a lock.
3. Add `#[ignore]` or `serial_test::serial` gate on the implicated tests only after the root cause is understood (applying `serial` prematurely masks the bug instead of fixing it).

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace` from completing cleanly — the very first workspace-wide test abort halts CI before later crates (frankenlibc-core, frankenlibc-membrane, frankenlibc-harness, frankenlibc-bench) ever run. Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE pass.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` reproduces reliably on two distinct workers.
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test -- --test-threads=1` passes (172/172, 0.04s).
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:10:35.532200596Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T14:01:10.534830369Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T14:01:10.532970295Z","close_reason":"Scoped __cxa_thread_atexit_impl capture to local TLS dtor tests and revalidated glibc/startup ABI suites","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","critical","full-review","glibc","heap","race","test"]}

@@ -1389,6 +1389,50 @@ pub unsafe extern "C" fn __nss_passwd_lookup(
     -1
 }
 
+// __nss_lookup_function: resolve a per-service function pointer in
+// the NSS module loader. We have no NSS module loading (all our
+// passwd/group/hosts lookups go through dedicated abi modules), so
+// any caller asking for a function pointer gets NULL.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __nss_lookup_function(
+    _ni: *mut c_void,
+    _fct_name: *const c_char,
+) -> *mut c_void {
+    std::ptr::null_mut()
+}
+
+// __nss_hosts_lookup2: newer "v2" hosts lookup variant taking an
+// explicit address-family `type` and returning extended status via
+// `errnop`/`h_errnop`. Stubbed UNAVAIL for the same reason as
+// __nss_hosts_lookup.
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __nss_hosts_lookup2(
+    _nip: *mut *mut c_void,
+    _name: *const c_char,
+    _type: c_int,
+    _result: *mut c_void,
+    _errnop: *mut c_int,
+    _h_errnop: *mut c_int,
+) -> c_int {
+    -1
+}
+
+// __nss_next2: advance to next NSS service. Same UNAVAIL stub as
+// __nss_next, but with the additional `fct2_name` selector glibc
+// added for fallback-name resolution. We have no module chain so
+// the answer is always "no more services".
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __nss_next2(
+    _ni: *mut *mut c_void,
+    _fct_name: *const c_char,
+    _fct2_name: *const c_char,
+    _fctp: *mut *mut c_void,
+    _status: *mut c_int,
+    _all_values: c_int,
+) -> c_int {
+    -1
+}
+
 // ==========================================================================
 // __nl_langinfo_l and locale internals (4 symbols)
 // ==========================================================================
